item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see a couple other people have already commented that this shouldn't be "Safe" by modern definitions of object classes. This one doesn't even feel like a close call to me, but I am still learning the system, so I'm open to explanations.

If you're reading this comment and you believe Safe *is* the correct designation, can you please reply to me and explain why? You don't have to be the scip's author; I'll hear anybody out. (For the record, I personally do not mind if you "necro" in order to reply to this question. I'm not a mod, I'm just an ordinary reader; please don't break site rules for my sake; however, I have seen mods comment elsewhere that necro replies can be acceptable when they further a conversation productively.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the exact plot to a short in Simpsons tree-house of horrors 9
This is bizarre. Have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Predictable" A man got pregnant.
A man got pregnant

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love a good ATM-mimic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, technically they can, it's just the box would have to cover an area 50 square kilometers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I admit it, you had my +1 with the man-eating ATM's.

item.title: Oil
item.content:encoded:
Ok, so the North American Confederation, controlling an area in which our America is located in, occupied an area called Mesopotamia, which has Iraq in it.

Good to see there’s still oil in this universe.

item.title: Small Suggestion
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so this probably isn't going to get changed since this skip has been around so long, but just for my brain, I feel like I should include this to give the right voice for the ad.

"The Quez" ad for penile growth changes voice midway through and while I absolutely love it and love this skip, that was the one tweak I'd suggest, because no ad anywhere tells you there is a "catch" to their product or the price, they just tell you the price and how low low low it is!

With that said, here is my recommendation:

Narrator: For a modest fee, we here at MonoRaptor would be willing to intercede with Quetzalcoatl for you. Big Q will get you all the thickness and girth you need.

Narrator: You might expect to pay three to four fingers, or have to fork over autonomy of your body between 5:00PM and Midnight on Thursdays for such an incredible member! But, the Quez will provide you with the erection of your dreams at the cost of a few memories.

Hissing: I will see the world through your eyes.

Narrator: But hey, you've got plenty of memories, am I right? Do you really need to remember all your snot-nosed childhood friends?

Narrator: Contact a MonoRaptor agent today. Rates subject to evaluation of individual cases.

The change keeps the ad styled to itself, encouraging you to work with this company with zero reservations on the price or its value to you. The "Here's the catch" line would make me pause and rethink getting my penis enlarged (if I had one) because the ad calls it a catch.

Just food for though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Intresting, a broadcast from an alternate world where anomalies are better understood and accepted. One where the SCP Foundation might not even exist

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not terribly keen; as people have said, it's a well-used trope, and to me this didn't add enough (possibly because I have never heard The Jerry Springer Show.

I would suggest one minor improvement for tone: put the name of the county in Florida at the top of the article in place of "panhandle", which sounds a little colloquial.
Is there an Aztec god that I can summon to be my friend?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe use the word "pinpointed" to make it clearer?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Though I disagree with Clockwork Punk on the reason, I'm also not impressed with the skip. I do see the quality of having a skip that isn't novel, but adds to the volume of good iterations of a concept. I do think that lighter skips are good to keep some balance, even to increase the horror factor of other skips (at a too bleak world, fear is meaningless).

But there are too many little excesses that just bug me in there. The Containment seems lacking, not that it should be too big or fancy, but as is it seems just lazy work. At least the effort to triangulate the source should be here.

Similarly, the clinical tone is off, too informal, and for no true gain, it wouldn't even change much the length of the article to make it more formal.

What bothers me more, though, are the somewhat frequent "tell, don't show" about how hard it is for the Foundation to do something about this thing at all. Seriously, most of those things don't need to be put there at all to keep the idea, and the very idea that this thing isn't contained is superfluous to the narrative. There is no need even to spell out that the transmission is from another dimension, it can be easily implied.

Cut out the 3rd and 5th paragraphs of the Description (not counting the log), rework the 6th to better deliver the punchline without putting an O5 in the mess, and maybe it will get an upvote from me.

item.title: Meh...
item.content:encoded: Ok so I know the majority of people are going to disagree with me but I simply didn’t like how it all played out, I would love if it had a bit more of a creep or scare factor, hell even if it was one sentence of something so creepy your skin crawls I would have upvoted. I’ll refrain from voting but this is my honest opinion

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was neutral on this one up until the last line.
"one might form a rather negative opinion of our own universe if the sole source of information about it was The Jerry Springer Show."
That was a good twist. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always love when a skip I'd nearly forgotten about gets featured.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Think this should be Euclid since you can’t necessarily put a box around it and leave it there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoy parallel universe SCPs a lot, and this doesn't disappoint. Especially ones where things are just different enough to be bizarre, but not so different that it's entirely unrecognizable.

Also that last line had me grinning like an idiot. Just that really nice deadpan line delivered clinically enough to not be tonally jarring but still feel like a break from sterility.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's so simple and the pacing is almost perfect. I think this may have just made my list of SCPs to recommend to people when they're asking for suggestions.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: "Paging Dr. …. Dr. Love to the Site Director's office for your daily debriefing. Dr. Love to the Site Director's office. Thank you."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, okay. I misunderstood the the first quote as meaning they had absolutely no idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They've figured out the general area of where it is, but within that area, they haven't found where it actually is. Did that help?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remote sensing has not revealed a physical source for SCP-3159.

…at present, the location of the transmission can only be localized to a 50km2 region within Okaloosa County, Florida.

Am I missing something or is this a contradiction?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm conflicted. On the one hand, I would love to hear more of this broadcasts. On the other hand, I would worry it would play itself out with too many more strange commercials. Either way I enjoyed this.
Probably couldn't tell you my own first name if I lived there. Hung like a moose, though.

item.title: Safe?
item.content:encoded:
"Safe-class objects are those anomalies that are either sufficiently understood that they are now completely and reliably contained on a permanent basis or otherwise do not trigger their anomalous effects unless intentionally activated."

This is currently uncontained; I wouldn't think it is Safe even if it's relatively harmless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1

Gotta disagree with the majority… while I tend to enjoy the "Transmissions from an alternate dimension," I didn't think it was executed well here. The Jerry Springer bit was boring and predictable. The only bit I found entertaining was the Quetzlcoatl ad, and that didn't do enough to save it. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The "weird version of a real life show" is a well worn trope, but I liked it all the same. It never got too daft and didn't outstay its welcome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This takes an old idea and makes it interesting again. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic. I especially love the last line, but those are some seriously creative commercials. XD I need to find someone who sounds like Jerry Springer now…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Articles like this live or die on how entertaining the logs are. These had me hooked and left me wanting more. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The premise of this has been done a whole lot, but the transcript was entertaining and didn't feel like a rehash of other "alternate universe that weird" articles. Also, that last line was a great note to end this on.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Moreover, it has also been suggested that one might form a rather negative opinion of our own universe if the sole source of information about it was The Jerry Springer Show.

Too true, that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a lot of fun - "transmission from another universe" is a very familiar premise, but you don't waste time overexplaining it, and the show/commercials are nailing the funny-unsettling vibe you seem to be going for. You don't water down the good stuff you have by adding weaker examples just for the sake of length, making this a nice play on a familiar trope that avoids wearing it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few weeks ago, I had a nightmare that I was being eaten alive by an ATM, with people on the street looking on and sadly shaking their heads, commiserating about the terribly high number of "flytraps" these days. That more-or-less motivated this skip.

Thanks a lot to Modern_Erasmus and Dr. Chandra for feedback in the drafts forum.
